source_name: Code4rena Kinetiq Findings and Analysis Report (2025-04) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
summary: The generatePerformance function skips inactive oracles without emitting an event, reducing transparency. This could allow malicious oracles to selectively participate, potentially skewing performance averages unnoticed.
root_cause: QA/low-risk implementation weakness described in the final C4 low/non-critical report.
why_not_medium_or_high: the issue is presented in the final report low/non-critical section and does not by itself establish a permissionless high-impact exploit or direct loss of principal assets
operational_risk: The generatePerformance function skips inactive oracles without emitting an event, reducing transparency. This could allow malicious oracles to selectively participate, potentially skewing performance averages unnoticed.
recommended_mitigation: Emit an event when skipping an inactive oracle to log the occurrence: event OracleSkipped(address indexed oracle, string reason); if (!oracle.isActive) { emit OracleSkipped(oracle.address, "Inactive oracle"); continue; }
when_it_could_escalate: if combined with permissionless access, stale oracle data, or large unbounded sets
if protocol configuration makes this path critical to solvency or withdrawal availability
